text: I find myself on the threshold between two realms: the quiet orderliness of a glass-walled meeting room and the vivid, stimulating colors of an exhibit dedicated to research and development. In the first space, I sense anticipation—the emptiness of the room invites future dialogue and possibility, yet feels sterile and expectant. In the second, the environment teems with creative potential and energy; the bold hues and display of screens and text evoke curiosity and a certain urgency to learn and innovate. Here, surrounded by the artifacts of human curiosity and technical achievement, I feel a subtle awe—an energizing reminder of how our ideas reshape reality. Being present, I am both observer and participant, quietly shaped by the atmosphere of innovation and the hope it embodies.
